mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon as investors price in stronger demand.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% fails to produce positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of paired observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor markets and rising recession risk, depressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If conditional on trigger, the median 60-day forward return is not negative (one-sided Wilcoxon test p > 0.10) over the available sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation (CPIAUCSL above 3%) and falling housing starts (HOUST below 1200K) indicate stagflationary pressures, eroding real earnings growth and causing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the joint condition produces positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions (real GDP growth positive), rising industrial production (INDPRO) confirms healthy output growth, boosting earnings expectations and generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
regime_hint: economic expansion (real GDP growth positive over past year)
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.2% fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 70% of expansion periods identified by trailing GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, further upward pressure on the rate signals aggressive tightening that compresses valuation multiples, leading to negative 5-day S&P 500 returns as markets price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return conditional on FEDFUNDS > 5% is not lower than the unconditional median (Mann-Whitney test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High payroll employment growth (PAYEMS above 155M) combined with a federal funds rate above 4% signals an overheated labor market that will require additional tightening, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
regime_hint: tight labor market with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: If the joint trigger condition does not result in a negative mean 20-day return significant at the 10% level over all historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect underlying economic momentum and improved capacity utilization, driving positive 60-day S&P 500 returns as investors anticipate sustained profit growth.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO 3-month change and subsequent 60-day return is zero or negative ( Spearman's rho <= 0) over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail sales (RSAFS) exceed 700 billion dollars, consumer spending strength signals robust economic health, leading to short-term positive 5-day S&P 500 returns as traders react to the economic release.
failure_condition: If the 5-day return following the trigger is not positive in at least 60% of cases over the data history.
